action.reasoning: Observation: Iteration 28 failed during a smoke test for mega_best_v2.pt because the checkpoint contained numpy scalar types incompatible with torch.load's default weights_only=True. The best test balanced_accuracy remains 0.8544 from iteration 26 (ensemble_ft.pt). Analysis: Instead of debugging the corrupted checkpoint, I can leverage the fact that all 6 trained models have cached flip-TTA validation logits available. A fast, exhaustive ensemble search over all pairs, triples, and selected 4-way combinations using these precomputed logits requires no neural network forward passes and will complete in under a minute. By ensuring the saved ensemble checkpoint uses only native Python floats and torch Tensors (no numpy types), predict.py will load it successfully. Decision: Write final_ensemble.py to load all cached logits, perform an exhaustive grid search over ensemble weights and logit-adjustment temperature, save the best clean ensemble checkpoint, run a smoke test, and immediately submit it for evaluation.
